recommendations: rg_replace_flag_bundle: prop-20260612-110331-da hook enforcement proposal has been pending across 6 confirmed recurrences spanning multiple sessions. No new advisory recommendation warranted — three prior cycles have already recommended the same hook; repeating the recommendation without implementation evidence is noise. Flag as stalled rather than re-recommending.

shell_tool_preference_violations: zero improvement trajectory across all observed cycles; prior advisory cycles have had no effect. A PreToolUse hook blocking Bash calls whose sole substantive operation is sed with line-range flags or cat piped to head on a local file path is the only candidate prevention mechanism not yet tried. No new advisory text warranted beyond what prior cycles have already stated.

infra_before_grep: ATONE self-filing now occurs reliably on detection (positive signal), but the initial violation still precedes detection and requires user correction. The rule exists; the check is not happening before the build decision. A lightweight PreToolUse hook that fires on Write or TaskCreate when the target path pattern matches a 'workaround inbox' shape (placeholders, manifests) could intercept the build step and prompt a broader grep first.
